sources.title: NCT04683926 registry
sources.access_scope: registration facts, status, and aggregate results

sources.title: NCT04683926 protocol
sources.access_scope: design, eligibility, dosing, schedule, and protocol statistics

sources.title: NCT04683926 statistical_analysis_plan
sources.access_scope: analysis populations, safety outputs, and CRF time-axis mapping

facts.name: protocol number
facts.value: OMNI-PAIN-103

facts.name: clinical phase
facts.value: Phase 1

facts.name: study population
facts.value: healthy men and women aged 19 to 55

facts.name: study design
facts.value: open-label randomized balanced single-dose four-treatment four-period four-sequence Williams-square crossover

facts.name: treatments
facts.value: 10 mg fasted; 20 mg fasted; 30 mg fasted; 30 mg fed

facts.name: protocol dose days
facts.value: Day 1; Day 4; Day 7; Day 10

facts.name: screening window
facts.value: Protocol <=30 days; ICF up to 31 days

facts.name: study or confinement duration
facts.value: Protocol/SAP 11 days; ICF about 12 days

facts.name: study objectives
facts.value: dose proportionality; 30 mg food effect; safety and tolerability

facts.name: key PK endpoints
facts.value: M1 AUC0-inf; AUC0-t when appropriate; Cmax; T1/2; Tmax; Tlag

facts.name: fasting requirement
facts.value: at least 10 h before dose and at least 4 h after dose

facts.name: fed treatment meal
facts.value: high-fat high-calorie meal started about 30 min before dose and consumed within 20 min

facts.name: water restriction
facts.value: Synopsis and SAP: no water 1 h before/after; Protocol body: only water allowed 1 h before/after

facts.name: safety population
facts.value: all participants receiving at least one dose

facts.name: per-protocol population
facts.value: meets eligibility; completes Day 11; receives all four doses; at least 13 of 15 PK samples per dose

facts.name: evaluable population
facts.value: calculable AUC0-inf or AUC0-t and/or Cmax; used for dose proportionality and food effect in Protocol

facts.name: Protocol time axis
facts.value: continuous study Day -1 through Day 11

facts.name: CRF/SAP time axis
facts.value: four periods; each dose represented as Day 1 within its period

facts.name: interim analysis
facts.value: no interim analysis

facts.name: power statement
facts.value: 24 participants; 20% intra-subject CV; 5% significance; about 89.6% power

facts.name: participants analyzed by treatment
facts.value: 10 mg fasted 24; 20 mg fasted 23; 30 mg fasted 23; 30 mg fed 23

facts.name: study status
facts.value: Completed

sections.title: Protocol Synopsis
sections.text: Participants receive treatments on Study Days 1, 4, 7, and 10 with a washout interval of greater than 3 days. Participants remain in the study for 11 days. No water is allowed one hour before and one hour after dosing. Nominal PK samples are collected at 0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 2.5, 3, 3.5, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 24, and 32 hours after each dose. Up to 32 participants may be randomized to obtain 24 PK-complete participants.

sections.title: Protocol Study Design and Procedures
sections.text: The four doses are administered on continuous Study Days 1, 4, 7, and 10, providing 3 days between doses. During the period from one hour before to one hour after dosing, only water is allowed. The study runs from Day -1 through Day 11. The final nominal PK sample is obtained 32 hours post-dose.

sections.title: Protocol Appendix A Schedule of Procedures
sections.text: The schedule uses one continuous Day -1 to Day 11 study timeline. PK sampling for each treatment includes a final 32-hour time point.

sections.title: Protocol Analysis Populations
sections.text: The Safety Population includes all participants who receive at least one dose. The Per-Protocol Population must meet eligibility, complete Day 11, receive all four doses, and provide at least 13 of 15 PK samples per dose. The Evaluable Population has calculable AUC0-inf or AUC0-t and/or Cmax and is used for dose proportionality and food-effect analyses.

sections.title: SAP Study Design and Scope
sections.text: Participants remain in the study for 11 days. No water is allowed one hour before and one hour after dosing. The SAP is limited to safety analysis. The CRF represents the crossover as four separate periods, with dosing recorded as Day 1 within each period, while the Protocol uses continuous Days 1, 4, 7, and 10.

sections.title: SAP PK Dataset and TFL Specification
sections.text: PK records use period-specific nominal time points through 32 hours. Population mapping and PK completeness rules require statistical review because the SAP's stated scope is safety analysis.

sections.title: ICF Study Participation and Burden
sections.text: Screening may occur up to 31 days before dosing. Participants will stay at the research unit for about 12 days and undergo blood sampling extending through the final 32-hour post-dose collection.

sections.title: Protocol Screening Window
sections.text: Screening is performed no more than 30 days before dosing.

sections.title: Synthetic CRF Nominal PK Time Field
sections.text: For prototype testing, each period has its own Day 1 and allows nominal PK times through 32 hours. This CRF representation is synthetic and is not a real study record.

sections.title: ClinicalTrials.gov Registration and Results
sections.text: NCT04683926 is completed with actual enrollment of 24. Aggregate result time frames and PK descriptions are currently aligned to the public 32-hour sampling schedule.
